scene_title: Camp near Sardis. Before Brutus's tent

lines.speaker: BRUTUS
lines.text: Stand, ho!

lines.speaker: LUCILIUS
lines.text: Give the word, ho! and stand.

lines.speaker: BRUTUS
lines.text: What now, Lucilius! is Cassius near?

lines.speaker: LUCILIUS
lines.text: He is at hand; and Pindarus is come

lines.speaker: LUCILIUS
lines.text: To do you salutation from his master.

lines.speaker: BRUTUS
lines.text: He greets me well. Your master, Pindarus,

lines.speaker: BRUTUS
lines.text: In his own change, or by ill officers,

lines.speaker: BRUTUS
lines.text: Hath given me some worthy cause to wish

lines.speaker: BRUTUS
lines.text: Things done, undone: but, if he be at hand,

lines.speaker: BRUTUS
lines.text: I shall be satisfied.

lines.speaker: PINDARUS
lines.text: I do not doubt

lines.speaker: PINDARUS
lines.text: But that my noble master will appear

lines.speaker: PINDARUS
lines.text: Such as he is, full of regard and honour.

lines.speaker: BRUTUS
lines.text: He is not doubted. A word, Lucilius;

lines.speaker: BRUTUS
lines.text: How he received you, let me be resolved.

lines.speaker: LUCILIUS
lines.text: With courtesy and with respect enough;

lines.speaker: LUCILIUS
lines.text: But not with such familiar instances,

lines.speaker: LUCILIUS
lines.text: Nor with such free and friendly conference,

lines.speaker: LUCILIUS
lines.text: As he hath used of old.

lines.speaker: BRUTUS
lines.text: Thou hast described

lines.speaker: BRUTUS
lines.text: A hot friend cooling: ever note, Lucilius,

lines.speaker: BRUTUS
lines.text: When love begins to sicken and decay,

lines.speaker: BRUTUS
lines.text: It useth an enforced ceremony.

lines.speaker: BRUTUS
lines.text: There are no tricks in plain and simple faith;

lines.speaker: BRUTUS
lines.text: But hollow men, like horses hot at hand,

lines.speaker: BRUTUS
lines.text: Make gallant show and promise of their mettle;

lines.speaker: BRUTUS
lines.text: But when they should endure the bloody spur,

lines.speaker: BRUTUS
lines.text: They fall their crests, and, like deceitful jades,

lines.speaker: BRUTUS
lines.text: Sink in the trial. Comes his army on?

lines.speaker: LUCILIUS
lines.text: They mean this night in Sardis to be quarter’d;

lines.speaker: LUCILIUS
lines.text: The greater part, the horse in general,

lines.speaker: LUCILIUS
lines.text: Are come with Cassius.

lines.speaker: BRUTUS
lines.text: Hark! he is arrived. Low march within.

lines.speaker: BRUTUS
lines.text: March gently on to meet him.

lines.speaker: CASSIUS
lines.text: Stand, ho!

lines.speaker: BRUTUS
lines.text: Stand, ho! Speak the word along.

lines.speaker: FIRST SOLDIER
lines.text: Stand!

lines.speaker: SECOND SOLDIER
lines.text: Stand!

lines.speaker: THIRD SOLDIER
lines.text: Stand!

lines.speaker: CASSIUS
lines.text: Most noble brother, you have done me wrong.

lines.speaker: BRUTUS
lines.text: Judge me, you gods! wrong I mine enemies?

lines.speaker: BRUTUS
lines.text: And, if not so, how should I wrong a brother?

lines.speaker: CASSIUS
lines.text: Brutus, this sober form of yours hides wrongs;

lines.speaker: CASSIUS
lines.text: And when you do them—

lines.speaker: BRUTUS
lines.text: Cassius, be content;

lines.speaker: BRUTUS
lines.text: Speak your griefs softly: I do know you well.

lines.speaker: BRUTUS
lines.text: Before the eyes of both our armies here,

lines.speaker: BRUTUS
lines.text: Which should perceive nothing but love from us,

lines.speaker: BRUTUS
lines.text: Let us not wrangle: bid them move away;

lines.speaker: BRUTUS
lines.text: Then in my tent, Cassius, enlarge your griefs,

lines.speaker: BRUTUS
lines.text: And I will give you audience.

lines.speaker: CASSIUS
lines.text: Pindarus,

lines.speaker: CASSIUS
lines.text: Bid our commanders lead their charges off

lines.speaker: CASSIUS
lines.text: A little from this ground.

lines.speaker: BRUTUS
lines.text: Lucilius, do you the like; and let no man

lines.speaker: BRUTUS
lines.text: Come to our tent till we have done our conference.

lines.speaker: BRUTUS
lines.text: Let Lucius and Titinius guard our door.

stage_directions.text: Drum. Enter Brutus, Lucilius, Lucius, and Soldiers; Titinius and Pindarus meeting them.

glosses.definition: a loyal officer of Brutus; he will later impersonate Brutus at Philippi (5.4) to let Brutus escape
Cassius's freed-slave attendant; at 5.3 he kills Cassius on order

glosses.phrase: in his own change, or by ill officers
glosses.definition: either in his own altered disposition or through bad subordinates — Brutus diplomatically reserves judgment
intimate tokens; gestures of close friendship
a warm friend growing cold — Brutus's diagnosis; the friendship is ending
forced formality; stiff courtesy put on when real warmth fails
horses fiery when held in (at the start) but fading under pressure
spirit; temper of character; pun on metal
the spur drawing blood — real test under stress
drop their crests (like losing horses); give up
ancient city in Asia Minor (western Turkey); Brutus and Cassius's winter camp before Philippi
state your grievances at length (not 'make them larger')

annotations.title: 'A hot friend cooling': Brutus's diagnostic
annotations.body:
Brutus's four-line speech on the phenomenology of cooling friendship is the best short meditation on male political intimacy in Shakespeare. 'When love begins to sicken and decay, / It useth an enforced ceremony. / There are no tricks in plain and simple faith.' The image — formality as the symptom of emotional withdrawal — is one Shakespeare will use again in *Antony and Cleopatra*, but here it is put in the mouth of the warmer of the two men, and specifically as a diagnosis of the cooler.

The horse-metaphor that follows (hot-at-hand horses that 'fall their crests' under the bloody spur) is military, technical, and slightly cruel — Brutus is telling Lucilius that Cassius under pressure will fail, that the promise of mettle will not hold. The scene establishes the emotional register of 4.3: these men love each other, and they know the love is endangered, and they are saying so before they quarrel. Shakespeare's specific care here is that Brutus is not surprised by the quarrel that follows. He has been expecting it. The tent scene is the quarrel Brutus saw coming.

trials.prompt: What does Brutus mean by 'a hot friend cooling'?
options: A diagnosis of failing friendship — when love 'sickens and decays,' it shows up as 'enforced ceremony'; formality is the symptom of withdrawal

trials.prompt: 4.2's emotional register prepares us for 4.3 by:
options: Establishing that Brutus has seen the quarrel coming — he knows the warmth has begun to cool, and the tent scene that follows is the confrontation he has already diagnosed
